paper.title: The Binary Fraction of B-type Runaway Stars from LAMOST DR8

extraction.summary: The paper studies the binary fraction of 203 Galactic B-type runaway stars. All objects are explicitly classified as runaway stars (peculiar space velocity >43 km/s) escaping from their birth clusters, with no discussion of Galactic-unbound or hypervelocity status. No HVS/unbound candidates are included.

candidate_groups_considered.description: The RVs of the Runaway Star Sample (Table 1/tab:catalogs). Contains 203 B-type runaway stars with RV measurements from LAMOST DR8.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: The paper presents these objects as runaway stars (peculiar space velocity >43 km/s) ejected from their birth clusters, not as candidates possibly unbound from the Milky Way. The discussion focuses on binary fraction and formation mechanisms (BSS/DES) within the Galaxy, with no assessment of Galactic escape or bound/unbound status.

source_refs.context: Introduction defining runaway stars as stars escaping from parent clusters with peculiar velocities exceeding ~30-40 km/s, not Galactic-unbound objects.
Data section stating the sample consists of Galactic B-type runaway stars identified with runaway criterion peculiar space velocity >43 km/s. No discussion of Galactic escape or unbound status.

candidate_groups_considered.description: Properties of the Parameters and Variables Used in our Monte Carlo Simulation (Table 2/tab:parameters). Lists simulation parameters for binary fraction modeling, not individual stellar objects.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: This table contains Monte Carlo simulation parameters (masses, periods, mass ratios, eccentricities) rather than individual stellar objects or candidate lists. It is not a candidate table.

source_refs.context: Table caption and content showing this is a simulation parameter table, not an object catalog.
